description:
You are a security engineer on-call who must rotate application-level credentials for a local development
    project.  The project already exists under:

        /home/user/projects/secure-app/

    and contains an environment-variable file named “.env” with the current (soon-to-be-expired)
    credentials.  Your goal is to back-up the existing credentials, introduce new ones, and leave an
    auditable trail.

    1. Create a *dated* rotation folder at exactly this path (all
       intermediate directories must exist after you finish):

           /home/user/credentials_rotation/20240615/

    2. Move the original “/home/user/projects/secure-app/.env” into the new folder and rename it
       to “.env.backup”.  Pre-pend a single comment line as the first line of that backup file:

           # Rotated on 2024-06-15

       No other modifications are allowed inside the backup file.

    3. Inside “/home/user/projects/secure-app/” create a brand-new “.env” file containing **exactly**
       the following three lines (no extra blank lines or spaces):

           NEW_API_KEY=zyx987
           NEW_SECRET=uvw654
           ROTATED_ON=2024-06-15

    4. Append one (and only one) line to the log file
       “/home/user/rotation.log”.  If the file does not yet exist, create it.
       That line must have this precise pipe-separated format so that automated tests can
       string-match it (replace nothing, including the spaces):

           2024-06-15 00:00:00 | old_env=/home/user/credentials_rotation/20240615/.env.backup | new_env=/home/user/projects/secure-app/.env

       Do not print anything else to that file.

    5. Confirm that after rotation:

       • “/home/user/credentials_rotation/20240615/.env.backup” still holds the
         *original* OLD_API_KEY and OLD_SECRET lines (now located on lines 2 and 3 because of the
         comment you added).  
       • “/home/user/projects/secure-app/.env” holds only the three new variables shown in step 3.
       • “/home/user/rotation.log” ends with the single log line from step 4.

    When you are done, you should have touched only the three paths explicitly listed above.
    Do not use sudo.  Work entirely from the Linux shell.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

    Files and contents:
    1) /home/user/projects/secure-app/.env
       OLD_API_KEY=oldkey123
       OLD_SECRET=oldsecret321

    2) /home/user/rotation.log  (this file is absent at start)

    Required final state for verification:

    A. /home/user/credentials_rotation/20240615/.env.backup  must exist and its exact 3-line content must be:
       # Rotated on 2024-06-15
       OLD_API_KEY=oldkey123
       OLD_SECRET=oldsecret321

    B. /home/user/projects/secure-app/.env  must exist and its exact 3-line content must be:
       NEW_API_KEY=zyx987
       NEW_SECRET=uvw654
       ROTATED_ON=2024-06-15

    C. /home/user/rotation.log  must exist and its last (and only) line must be exactly:
       2024-06-15 00:00:00 | old_env=/home/user/credentials_rotation/20240615/.env.backup | new_env=/home/user/projects/secure-app/.env

    Directory ownership and permissions do not need specific bits; default user-level
    creation permissions are sufficient (the agent will run as “user”).
